title: citrus × aurantium subsp. bergamia
intro: Citrus × aurantium subsp. bergamia, also known as Bergamot, is a plant species in the Rutaceae family, characterized by its small, greenish-yellow flowers and distinctive citrus fruit. The essential oil extracted from the peel of Bergamot fruit has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Bergamot oil include reducing stress and anxiety, improving mood, and supporting digestive health, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. Bergamot oil is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as aromatherapy, skincare products, and teas, to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and soothe digestive issues.
